message:
fix(tests): Avoid holding MutexGuard across macro assertions

The assert_with_log! macro expands to code that may hold references
longer than intended. When a MutexGuard is passed directly, this can
cause subtle issues or clippy warnings about significant drops.

Fix by cloning the data before the assertion and dropping the guard:
- Clone the locked data into a local variable in a nested scope
- Pass the owned clone to the assertion macro
- Explicitly drop where needed for clarity

This pattern ensures the lock is released before any macro-generated
code runs, preventing potential deadlocks in test scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
